content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>https://github.com/nan556/claude-sync.git 这个仓库啊 你先查看readme 然后同步 我前面已经同步了几次了</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-05-16</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on durable technical signal:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)
- Concrete debugging or investigative findings from logs, traces, queue state, database rows, and code-path inspection

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored, discovered, confirmed, traced

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built or learned):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"
- "Observation queue for claude-mem session timed out waiting for an agent pool slot"
- "Fallback processing abandoned pending messages after Gemini and OpenRouter returned 404"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings with no follow-on finding
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- File related research that comes back empty or not found

If skipping, return an empty response only. Do not explain the skip in prose.

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

content.thinking:
Let me analyze these tool use messages:

1. First bash - checking if remote repo exists - confirmed it exists with HEAD at commit 81ea3fc3ee778c50bccf1231e6b546063cc387da
2. Second bash - cloned the repo to /tmp/claude-sync
3. Third bash - listing repo contents

The repo structure includes:
- .gitignore
- CLAUDE.md
- README.md (2656 bytes)
- history.jsonl (119KB - contains history data)
- minimax-skills-temp/
- monitor/
- plugins/
- projects/
- sessions/
- settings.json
- settings.local.json
- skills/
- sync.conf
- sync.sh (executable)
- uninstall.sh (executable)

This appears to be a Claude Code sync utility with multiple components. The repo seems to be a tool for syncing Claude configurations across sessions.

Let me create an observation capturing what was learned about the repository structure.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Claude-sync repository structure discovered</title>
  <subtitle>Repository contains sync utility for Claude configurations with plugins, skills, projects, and sessions</subtitle>
  <facts>

    <fact>history.jsonl is 119KB containing sync history data</fact>
    <fact>Also has minimax-skills-temp/ directory</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Claude-sync repository cloned and analyzed. It's a sync utility for Claude Code configurations with executable sync.sh and uninstall.sh scripts. The repository contains configuration files, plugins directory, skills directory, projects directory, sessions directory, and a monitor directory. The history.jsonl file contains 119KB of sync history data. Remote repo verified accessible at commit 81ea3fc3.</narrative>
```

  <working_directory>/mnt/d/assistant</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"/tmp/claude-sync/README.md\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/tmp/claude-sync/README.md\",\"content\":\"# Claude Code Sync\\n\\n跨设备同步 Claude Code 配置的脚本工具。\\n\\n## 功能\\n\\n- 将 `.claude` 配置目录同步到 Git 远程仓库\\n- 自动管理 session 文件（只保留最近的 N 个）\\n- 多设备间同步 Claude Code 配置和会话历史\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 新设备安装\\n\\n### 前提条件\\n\\n- 已安装 Git\\n- 已配置 SSH 公钥（用于访问 GitHub）\\n\\n### 第一步：备份现有配置（如有）\\n\\n```bash\\n# 如果已有 .claude 目录，先备份\\nmv ~/.claude ~/.claude.bak\\n```\\n\\n### 第二步：克隆仓库到 ~/.claude\\n\\n```bash\\ngit clone git@github.com:nan556/claude-sync.git ~/.claude\\ncd ~/.claude\\n```\\n\\n### 第三步：配置 Git 用户（如果需要）\\n\\n```bash\\ngit config user.name \\\"Your Name\\\"\\ngit config user.email \\\"your@email.com\\\"\\n```\\n\\n### 第四步：初始化 git hooks（如需自动同步）\\n\\n```bash\\n# 创建 post-commit hook\\ncat > .git/hooks/post-commit << 'EOF'\\n#!/bin/bash\\n~/.claude/sync.sh\\nEOF\\nchmod +x .git/hooks/post-commit\\n```\\n\\n### 第五步：首次同步\\n\\n```bash\\n# 拉取远程最新配置\\ngit pull\\n\\n# 手动运行同步确认正常\\n./sync.sh --verbose\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 配置说明\\n\\n### sync.conf\\n\\n```bash\\nKEEP_SESSIONS=3   # 每个项目保留的 session 文件数量\\n```\\n\\n根据需要调整保留的 session 数量。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 使用方法\\n\\n### 手动同步\\n\\n```bash\\n# 同步到远程\\n./sync.sh\\n\\n# 显示详细输出\\n./sync.sh --verbose\\n```\\n\\n### 自动化同步\\n\\n#### 方式一：cron 定时任务\\n\\n```bash\\n# 编辑 crontab\\ncrontab -e\\n\\n# 添加以下内容（每小时同步一次）\\n0 * * * * ~/.claude/sync.sh\\n```\\n\\n#### 方式二：Git hooks 自动推送\\n\\n每次 commit 后自动同步：\\n\\n```bash\\n# 创建 post-commit hook\\ncat > .git/hooks/post-commit << 'EOF'\\n#!/bin/bash\\n~/.claude/sync.sh\\nEOF\\nchmod +x .git/hooks/post-commit\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 工作原理\\n\\n1. `git pull` — 拉取远程更新，解决合并冲突（优先保留本地）\\n2. 扫描 `projects/*/` 目录下的 `.jsonl` session 文件\\n3. 每个项目目录只保留最近 N 个 session 文件\\n4. `git add` — 暂存变更\\n5. `git commit` + `git push` — 推送到远程\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 注意事项\\n\\n- session 文件按修改时间排序，保留最新的 N 个\\n- 较旧的 session 文件从 git 索引移除（本地文件仍保留）\\n- 首次使用建议先手动运行 `./sync.sh --verbose` 确认无误\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 项目结构\\n\\n```\\n~/.claude/\\n├── sync.sh        # 同步主脚本\\n├── sync.conf      # 配置文件\\n├── projects/      # Claude Code 项目会话\\n├── sessions/      # 全局会话记录\\n├── skills/        # 自定义技能\\n├── plugins/       # 插件配置\\n└── settings.json  # Claude Code 设置\\n```\\n\",\"numLines\":145,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":145}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Claude-sync tool functionality documented</title>
  <subtitle>Cross-device sync utility for Claude Code configurations with session management</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Sync utility clones to ~/.claude directory</fact>
    <fact>sync.conf controls KEEP_SESSIONS count per project (default N=3)</fact>
    <fact>sync.sh workflow: git pull → scan projects/*.jsonl → keep N sessions → git add/commit/push</fact>
    <fact>Supports cron-based automation (hourly sync) and git post-commit hooks</fact>
    <fact>Auto-sync setup via .git/hooks/post-commit</fact>
    <fact>Conflicts resolved by preferring local changes</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Claude-sync is a cross-device synchronization tool for Claude Code configurations. Installed by cloning to ~/.claude. The sync.sh script manages session files per project, keeping only the most recent N sessions (configured in sync.conf). Supports both cron-based scheduled sync and git hooks for automatic push after commits. Old session files are removed from git index (locally retained). Setup requires SSH keys for GitHub access and git config user settings.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>/tmp/claude-sync/README.md</file>
    <file>/tmp/claude-sync/sync.conf</file>
    <file>/tmp/claude-sync/sync.sh</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-05-16T08:35:52.182Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>/mnt/d/assistant</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"/tmp/claude-sync/sync.sh\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/tmp/claude-sync/sync.sh\",\"content\":\"#!/usr/bin/env bash\\n# cc-sync: Claude Code configuration sync script\\n# https://github.com/ikook-wang/cc-sync\\n\\nCLAUDE_DIR=\\\"$HOME/.claude\\\"\\nCONF_FILE=\\\"$CLAUDE_DIR/sync.conf\\\"\\nVERBOSE=false\\n\\n# Parse arguments\\nfor arg in \\\"$@\\\"; do\\n  case \\\"$arg\\\" in\\n    --verbose|-v) VERBOSE=true ;;\\n  esac\\ndone\\n\\nlog() { $VERBOSE && echo \\\"[cc-sync] $*\\\"; }\\n\\n# Read config\\nKEEP_SESSIONS=3\\nif [ -f \\\"$CONF_FILE\\\" ]; then\\n  # shellcheck source=/dev/null\\n  . \\\"$CONF_FILE\\\"\\nfi\\n\\ncd \\\"$CLAUDE_DIR\\\" || exit 0\\n\\n# Ensure we're in a git repo with a remote\\ngit rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree &>/dev/null || exit 0\\ngit remote get-url origin &>/dev/null || exit 0\\n\\nlog \\\"Pulling remote changes...\\\"\\ngit pull --no-rebase --autostash --quiet 2>/dev/null || {\\n  log \\\"Merge conflict detected, auto-resolving with local version...\\\"\\n  git checkout --ours . 2>/dev/null\\n  git add -A 2>/dev/null\\n  git commit -m \\\"sync: auto-resolve merge conflicts\\\" --quiet 2>/dev/null || true\\n}\\n\\nlog \\\"Processing session files (keeping $KEEP_SESSIONS per project)...\\\"\\n\\n# Iterate over each project directory\\nfor project_dir in projects/*/; do\\n  [ -d \\\"$project_dir\\\" ] || continue\\n\\n  # Find .jsonl files sorted by modification time (newest first)\\n  # Compatible with bash 3.2 (no mapfile)\\n  jsonl_files=()\\n  while IFS= read -r file; do\\n    [ -n \\\"$file\\\" ] && jsonl_files+=(\\\"$file\\\")\\n  done < <(find \\\"$project_dir\\\" -maxdepth 1 -name \\\"*.jsonl\\\" -exec ls -t {} + 2>/dev/null)\\n\\n  [ ${#jsonl_files[@]} -eq 0 ] && continue\\n\\n  log \\\"  $project_dir: ${#jsonl_files[@]} sessions found\\\"\\n\\n  # Force-add the most recent N sessions\\n  for ((i=0; i<KEEP_SESSIONS && i<${#jsonl_files[@]}; i++)); do\\n    git add -f \\\"${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n    log \\\"    + ${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\"\\n\\n    # Also add the corresponding UUID directory (subagents, tool-results)\\n    uuid_dir=\\\"${jsonl_files[$i]%.jsonl}\\\"\\n    if [ -d \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" ]; then\\n      git add -f \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n    fi\\n  done\\n\\n  # Remove older sessions from git index only (keep local files)\\n  for ((i=KEEP_SESSIONS; i<${#jsonl_files[@]}; i++)); do\\n    if git ls-files --error-unmatch \\\"${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\" &>/dev/null; then\\n      git rm --cached --quiet \\\"${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n      log \\\"    - ${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\"\\n    fi\\n\\n    uuid_dir=\\\"${jsonl_files[$i]%.jsonl}\\\"\\n    if [ -d \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" ] && git ls-files --error-unmatch \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" &>/dev/null; then\\n      git rm -r --cached --quiet \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n    fi\\n  done\\ndone\\n\\n# Stage all other tracked files\\ngit add -A 2>/dev/null || true\\n\\n# Commit and push if there are changes\\nif ! git diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then\\n  log \\\"Committing changes...\\\"\\n  git commit -m \\\"sync\\\" --quiet 2>/dev/null || true\\nfi\\n\\nlog \\\"Pushing to remote...\\\"\\ngit push --quiet 2>/dev/null || true\\n\\nlog \\\"Done.\\\"\\n\",\"numLines\":95,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":95}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

do\\n    if git ls-files --error-unmatch \\\"${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\" &>/dev/null; then\\n      git rm --cached --quiet \\\"${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n      log \\\"    - ${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\"\\n    fi\\n\\n    uuid_dir=\\\"${jsonl_files[$i]%.jsonl}\\\"\\n    if [ -d \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" ] && git ls-files --error-unmatch \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" &>/dev/null; then\\n      git rm -r --cached --quiet \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n    fi\\n  done\\ndone\\n\\n# Stage all other tracked files\\ngit add -A 2>/dev/null || true\\n\\n# Commit and push if there are changes\\nif ! git diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; </observed_from_primary_session>

  <what_happened>Bash</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-05-16T08:36:02.229Z</occurred_at>
